evidence: import cowabunga_sdk as lfai used in 8 production files (routers/openai/{chat,completions,embeddings,audio}, routers/cowabunga/count, backend/grpc_client, backend/helpers, backend/rag/leapfrogai_embeddings)
get_session() backward-compatibility alias in database_factory.py has zero callers; all consumers use create_database_client() directly
leapfrogai_embeddings.py module filename retains old project name and is actively imported by index.py and query.py
lfai_models, lfai_vector_stores, lfai_token_count, lfai_rag, lfai_tokens local aliases in main.py disambiguate cowabunga routers using old brand prefix
TODO comment in converters.py references https://github.com/defenseunicorns/leapfrogai/issues/1110 (old project org)
protobuf generated files embed github.com/defenseunicorns/leapfrogai/ package paths (skipped as generated code)
incomplete_migration.issues_preventing_higher_score: The persistent lfai alias for cowabunga_sdk forces developers to mentally map an obsolete brand name across the API layer. Removing it would require a coordinated refactor of 8+ files and the associated test references.

incomplete_migration.dimension_character: The codebase carries localized but consistent residue from a rebrand leapfrogai→cowabunga. The SDK alias lfai, a stale backward-compatibility shim, and an old project name in a module filename are the main remnants. These do not break functionality but create persistent cognitive overhead for new developers.
incomplete_migration.score_rationale: A 76.0 reflects that the migration is functionally complete but the branding rename did not propagate to internal identifiers. The lfai alias is the dominant friction point. No mixed JS/TS modules, no deprecated symbols still called, and no stale migration TODOs were found. The issues are narrow and fixable with a single coordinated refactor.

issues.summary: cowabunga_sdk imported as lfai across API layer — incomplete rebrand from leapfrogai
evidence: 8 production files contain 'import cowabunga_sdk as lfai' or reference lfai.CompletionRequest, lfai.ChatItem, lfai.EmbeddingRequest, etc.
The alias forces every reader to mentally map 'lfai' (leapfrogai) to the current 'cowabunga_sdk' module name.
issues.suggestion: Remove the 'as lfai' alias and use 'cowabunga_sdk' directly (or a domain-neutral alias like 'sdk'). Update all call sites in a single coordinated change.

issues.summary: get_session() backward-compatibility alias has no callers and is documented as legacy shim
evidence: database_factory.py defines get_session() with docstring: 'This function maintains compatibility with existing code that expects a Supabase-like session.'
Zero callers found across the codebase; all consumers invoke create_database_client() directly.
issues.suggestion: Delete the get_session() function and its docstring. No other changes are needed.

issues.summary: leapfrogai_embeddings.py retains old project name in active module path
evidence: Module is named leapfrogai_embeddings.py but implements CowabungaAIEmbeddings class.
Imported by index.py and query.py as 'from cowabunga_api.backend.rag.leapfrogai_embeddings import CowabungaAIEmbeddings'.
issues.suggestion: Rename the file to cowabunga_embeddings.py (or embeddings_client.py) and update the two import sites.

issues.summary: main.py uses lfai_* local aliases for cowabunga routers instead of current naming
evidence: Lines 14-18 define imports: models as lfai_models, vector_stores as lfai_vector_stores, count as lfai_token_count, rag as lfai_rag, tokens as lfai_tokens.
Lines 116-121 register routers using these lfai-prefixed aliases.
issues.suggestion: Rename local aliases from lfai_* to cowabunga_* (e.g., cowabunga_models, cowabunga_vector_stores) to align with the current project name.

issues.summary: TODO comment references obsolete leapfrogai GitHub issue tracker
evidence: converters.py:70 contains '# TODO: What should these numbers be? https://github.com/defenseunicorns/leapfrogai/issues/1110'
issues.suggestion: Update the TODO to reference the current project issue tracker or repository, or resolve the open question and remove the TODO.

root_causes: Project rebrand from leapfrogai to cowabunga renamed top-level packages but did not propagate the change into internal import aliases, module filenames, or inline documentation.
likely_symptoms: stale backward-compatibility shim with no callers
old project name in active module paths
possible_false_positives: API key prefix 'lfai_' was considered but dismissed as external backward-compatibility (published API contract)
Protobuf generated files with leapfrogai paths were considered but dismissed as generated code per rubric skip list
build/ directory containing old leapfrogai_sdk references was considered but dismissed because the directory is already gitignored

add.description: The SDK module was renamed from leapfrogai to cowabunga_sdk, but 8 production files still import it as 'lfai'. This is an internal-only alias that creates persistent cognitive overhead without serving external compatibility.
database_factory.py defines get_session() explicitly as backward compatibility, yet zero callers exist. All code uses create_database_client() directly.

add.header: UI JS web components are intentional, not migration residue
add.description: The two .js files in the UI (CodeBlock.js, styles.js) are Lit-based web components with explicit documentation explaining why they are implemented as vanilla JS instead of Svelte/TS. This is not an incomplete TypeScript migration.
